notes: SHIP v1.14.205 — pt-btn-primary border-box->padding-box fix (owner-requested follow-up to the .204 flag). The primary cask (LOAD MASTER, exports, parse, etc.) used the border-box gradient-ring trick which does not reliably paint on a raw <button> on iOS — same class of bug web-Claude fixed on .ask in .204. Converted to the padding-box primitive: real 1.5px cyan ring (color-mix w/ --glow-border, one-knob law) + padding-box glass (no border-box layer) + inset top-sheen/depth/glow + the 0 0 20px ambient (dropped .28->.22 to sit under the ring). appearance:none kept explicit (redundant w/ .pt-btn base). SCOPE: primary ONLY per owner instruction. Ghost/secondary/qa-btn are also raw <button>s w/ border-box, BUT were device-verified rendering in .190/.191 (the ghost BACK chips) + .198 pass — left untouched (no churn on confirmed-good surface); if any later reads flat, same one-line primitive swap. See memory borderbox-glass-fails-on-buttons. ROLLBACK: revert this rule to the .204 border-box version. Gates: 4 blocks compile, braces balanced, CRLF, 3-stamp lockstep .204 -> .205. BATCH: appends to the open .198-.205 batch.
